name: ship-brig
summary: The ship's jail — iron-barred cells built against the hull, a guard's stool, rusted manacles, the smell of bilge and fear.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: narrow — cells on one side, guard lane on the other

primary_palette.note: a pair of cells facing a guard lane
the captain gets results here — not a regular brig

story_prompts: a prisoner offers information no one else could know
the brig is empty but freshly-used — where did the prisoner go?
a previously-loyal officer sits in the cell, awaiting tribunal
the prisoner isn't human and the cell wasn't built for them
anti_patterns: cells line one long wall — don't scatter them around the room
the guard lane stays clear — don't clutter it with chests or barrels
no windows — brigs are always below the waterline or center-hull
